title: chenopodium benthamii subsp. benthamii
intro: Chenopodium benthamii subsp. benthamii, also known as the 'Bentham's goosefoot', is a subspecies of plant in the Amaranthaceae family, commonly found in wetland and coastal areas. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. The leaves and stems of the Chenopodium benthamii subsp. benthamii plant can be used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support the immune system and alleviate respiratory issues. The medicinal properties of this plant have been used to treat various health conditions, including bronchitis, asthma, and skin irritations.
